seo.title: Transaction Surcharge for Restaurants — 0 € Fees | Gastro Master
seo.description: Pass payment fees (PayPal, credit card, Klarna) transparently to customers — legally compliant and automated. Top-selling add-on for restaurant owners. Learn more.

hero.desc: The transaction surcharge is an add-on for restaurant owners that automatically and transparently passes payment fees from PayPal, credit card, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and Klarna as a separate line item to customers. You receive your full net revenue.

howItWorks.title: Zero fee burden in 3 simple steps.
howItWorks.desc: Fully automated, transparent, and legally compliant — no extra effort for you or your staff. The transaction surcharge is directly integrated into the

steps.title: Fee arises at checkout
steps.text: Your customer selects a payment method — e.g. PayPal (2.99% + €0.35) or credit card via Stripe (1.5% + €0.25). The exact fee is calculated in real time.

steps.title: Surcharge shown transparently at checkout
steps.text: Before paying, the customer sees the fee as a separate line item — clearly labeled and comprehensible. No hidden charges, fully compliant.

steps.title: You receive 100% of your revenue
steps.text: Not a cent is lost. Your PayPal Business or Stripe account receives the full amount — the payment fee was covered by the customer.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions about the transaction surcharge for restaurants.
faq.desc: More questions? Call us or write to us — we respond within 24 hours.

items.q: Am I allowed to pass credit card fees to customers?
items.a: Yes. In Germany and the EU, passing on payment fees as a transparent service charge (transaction surcharge) is permitted, provided it is clearly displayed at checkout. The EU Directive 2015/2366 (PSD2) only prohibits hidden surcharges — an openly communicated fee is perfectly legal. The transaction surcharge is fully integrated into the [Gastro Master POS system](/produkte/kassensystem).

items.q: How does a transaction surcharge work in a restaurant?
items.a: When a customer pays (e.g. via PayPal), the applicable fee (e.g. 2.99% + €0.35) is automatically added as a separate line item to the order total and transparently displayed at checkout. The restaurant owner receives the full net amount — without deductions. The feature is directly integrated into the [Gastro Master order shop](/produkte/webshop).

items.q: How much does PayPal cost restaurants per transaction?
items.a: PayPal charges 2.99% of the transaction amount plus €0.35 per transaction for online payments in Germany (source: paypal.com/de/webapps/mpp/merchant-fees). At 300 orders with an average value of €30, this amounts to approximately €374 per month — fees that are fully passed on to customers with the transaction surcharge. The add-on is especially worthwhile for [restaurant owners starting their own delivery service](/loesungen/lieferservice-gruenden).

items.q: Is the transaction surcharge for credit cards legal?
items.a: Yes. Credit card fees may be passed on as a transparent surcharge, as long as they are clearly visible during the ordering process. The Gastro Master platform automatically displays the fee at checkout, meeting all legal requirements. All prices and add-on conditions can be found in the [full pricing overview](/preise).

items.q: Which payment methods can be surcharged?
items.a: All common payment methods are covered: PayPal, Visa, Mastercard, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and Klarna. You can choose which methods to activate — the surcharge only applies to those. Setup is done directly in your [Gastro Master order shop](/produkte/webshop) or your [custom ordering app](/produkte/app).

items.q: Does the transaction surcharge work across multiple locations?
items.a: Yes. The transaction surcharge can be set up across locations. Each location can be linked to its own payment accounts (PayPal Business, Stripe) — the surcharge is then automatically applied to all activated locations. Learn more on the [franchise page](/loesungen/franchise).

items.q: Do I need my own PayPal and Stripe accounts?
items.a: Yes. Payments go directly to your own PayPal Business and/or Stripe account — no middleman, no delays. This is a key advantage over third-party platforms: you always have access to your money. If you need help with setup, our team is happy to assist — just reach out via the [contact form](/kontakt).
